title: registry.mirror does not support rooted FQDN
body:
Moved from https://github.com/containers/image/issues/2948#issuecomment-3271223939

`/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/099-example.conf` with this content:

```
[[registry]]
prefix = "docker.io"
insecure = false
blocked = false
location = "docker.io"

[[registry.mirror]]
location = "my-mirror.example.com.:5500/"
```

Causes weird error message:

```
$ buildah from --pull debian:bullseye-slim 
Trying to pull docker.io/library/debian:bullseye-slim...
Error: unable to copy from source docker://debian:bullseye-slim: initializing source docker://debian:bullseye-slim: rewriting reference: invalid reference format
```

Same issue happens if I use `location = "docker.io"` in the `[[registry]]` section.


Rooted FQDN are useful, to skip DNS search list. This especially useful in k8s clusters with search list being very long and with high `ndots` value in `resolve.conf`. This not only improves performance (some dns clients might even timeout before finishing whole search) , it also queries less DNS servers and help prevent leaking names to unwanted DNS servers.

```
$ buildah  --version
buildah version 1.41.3 (image-spec 1.1.1, runtime-spec 1.2.1)
```

on Fefora 42, amd64.

```
containers-common-0.64.1-2.fc42.noarch
containers-common-extra-0.64.1-2.fc42.noarch
buildah-1.41.3-1.fc42.x86_64
podman-5.6.0-1.fc42.x86_64
```

From my experience, essentially all tools, libraries and programming languages do support trailing `.` in domain names and URL. This does include docker too.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

The format of image references is, both historically and intentionally, not _everything_ supported by DNS, and the cross-vendor reference specification also rejects dots in that position: https://github.com/distribution/reference/blob/727f80d42224f6696b8e1ad16b06aadf2c6b833b/regexp.go#L97C47-L97C66 

With the number of `distribution/reference` uses all over the Docker codebase, starting with the very first step of `docker image pull`, I can’t see how the trailing dots could be accepted by that, but, also, I didn’t try that in practice.

Anyway, given the number of various hand-rolled parsers of these strings, and how they get embedded into various other formats, I think it’s very risky to add any new syntax without a very strong benefit. And, AFAICT, best practice is to avoid DNS search lists if at all possible.

---

We should, at least, improve the error handling here. Preferably reject the entry already when parsing the config file; at the very least, in the `rewriting reference` error, include the failing string.

Well, I am not sure `location` is the image reference per-se. Unless it is done using some rewriting mechanisms in the library.

In docker, the registry mirror is not done using rewriting, but rather via URL:

`cat /etc/docker/daemon.json`

```json
{
  ...
  "registry-mirrors": [
    "https://nexus.example.org.:5500"
  ],
  ...
}
```


If not supported, a better error message during config parsing would be appreciated.
Thanks, that would explain things. The `location` in `registries.conf` truly is a prefix of a reference, and that reference is then parsed/interpreted/processed using the ordinary flow.
